title: U-Bar
text: U-Bar The U-Bar is a multi-functional control that identifies the current object, and is used to locate and open or select development objects. U-Bars are found in the IDE header and in the Resource Browser. The U-Bar has a defined syntax for specifying objects. For example: cpt:MY_COMPONENT/ent:AN_ENTITY.MODEL/fld:FLD1 Copy For more information, see U-Bar Syntax . Main U-Bar The Main U-Bar, located in the IDE header, is used to: Open development objects for editing. Navigate to a subobject or property, editor worksheet, or window. Display the URL of the current main development object. To open an object for editing, click Browse , then select the object type and enter an object name. As you type, suggested objects are listed in a drop-down box. To navigate to a specific property, code position, worksheet, and window, you can enter a URL that specifies the object and where it should be opened. For example: cpt:ORDERFRM/ent:ORDERS.SALES/fld:BTN_ADD_LINE/prop:USCRIPT/pos:3,4 worksheet=script window=2 Copy Resource Browser U-Bar The U-Bar in the Resource Browser is used to filter the objects shown in the current tab so that you can select and add them to the current object. In the Component Editor, you can use the U-Bar in the Model tab to search for modeled entities and browse the fields or sub-entities of a specific modeled entity. For more information, see Filter and Search for Objects in Resource Browser and Smart Suggestions and Filtering . Table 1. Related learning videos Title link Using U-Bar Was this topic helpful?
